ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class; no specific jasmine-flavoured less-sugar RTD subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers ready-to-drink green tea; no jasmine-specific or less-sugar variant subcode exists in the published hierarchy. Closest defensible match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for packaged jasmine green tea RTD identified in the published SNOMED CT browser as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 500 ml bottle of packaged jasmine green tea with less sugar, providing approximately 110 kcal and 26 g of carbohydrates per bottle.

nutrient_source.title: Jasmine Green Tea (Less Sugar) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D09020055 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened green tea RTD
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for jasmine green tea less-sugar RTD found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 47 is estimated from the closest analogous approved-source entry: sweetened green tea RTD beverage (GI ~47, low category) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) and corroborated by University of Sydney GI Database entries for lightly sweetened tea beverages. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (47 × 27.55) / 100 ≈ 13.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (5.24 g/100 ml) in a sugar-sweetened beverage; sodium is very low at 8 mg/100 ml
hypertension.guidance: Despite very low sodium (8 mg/100 ml), the added sugar content of 5.24 g/100 ml classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage; regular consumption of SSBs is independently associated with elevated blood pressure (AHA 2018), so individuals with hypertension should choose the no-sugar packaged variant or unsweetened brewed green tea.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 5.24 g sugar/100 ml and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar raises postprandial glucose rapidly
type2_diabetes.guidance: This packaged less-sugar jasmine green tea contains 5.24 g of sugar per 100 ml with no dietary fibre to attenuate glucose absorption; a 500 ml bottle delivers approximately 26 g of rapidly absorbed sugar, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes — the no-sugar packaged variant or unsweetened brewed green tea should be chosen instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar in a packaged beverage; zero saturated fat and very low sodium are positive factors, but SSB sugar intake is linked to dyslipidaemia and CVD risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While this beverage contains no fat and negligible sodium, its added sugar content (5.24 g/100 ml; ~26 g per 500 ml bottle) contributes to excess free sugar intake, which is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit SSB consumption and prefer the no-sugar variant.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar counteracts potential EGCG anti-inflammatory benefit; EGCG concentration in RTD packaged tea is typically insufficient for meaningful anti-inflammatory effect
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The added sugar in this packaged less-sugar jasmine green tea promotes pro-inflammatory mediators and likely negates any residual EGCG polyphenol benefit present in RTD formulations; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should choose the no-sugar packaged variant or freshly brewed jasmine green tea to maximise anti-inflammatory polyphenol intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jasmine Green Tea, Less Sugar (Packaged RTD) is a commercially produced, ready-to-drink bottled beverage sold widely in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is made from brewed or extracted green tea infused with jasmine flower flavouring, lightly sweetened with sugar to a reduced level compared with standard sweetened RTD teas. The liquid is clear to pale golden-green in colour with a delicate floral jasmine aroma and a mildly sweet, slightly astringent taste characteristic of green tea. Per 100 ml, it provides 22 kcal, 5.51 g of carbohydrates (of which 5.24 g is sugar), negligible protein and fat, and 8 mg of sodium. Dietary fibre is absent. A standard 500 ml bottle delivers approximately 110 kcal and 26 g of rapidly absorbed sugar. Clinically, this beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) despite its 'less sugar' labelling, as sugar exceeds 5 g per 100 ml. It is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to rapid postprandial glucose elevation from liquid sugar with no fibre buffer. For hypertension, the very low sodium is favourable but the SSB sugar content independently raises blood pressure risk. For cardiovascular disease, the added sugar contributes to excess free sugar intake linked to dyslipidaemia. Green tea polyphenols (EGCG) may be present in small amounts but RTD formulations typically contain lower concentrations than freshly brewed tea. The no-sugar packaged variant or unsweetened brewed jasmine green tea is the clinically preferred alternative for all health-sensitive populations. This product is widely consumed as a daily beverage in Singapore, Malaysia, and across East and Southeast Asia.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020055, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95). Exact match to the named food item. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008, which reduces GI confidence to medium for that field alone, but overall record confidence remains high given the exact HPB nutrient match.
notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB — this is a water-based tea beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB — no fat-containing ingredients are present in this formulation; saturated_fat_g is correspondingly confirmed zero. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB — brewed tea beverages contain no dietary fibre. Fields set to -1 (data not available in HPB source): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g — detailed micronutrient and fatty acid profiles for this packaged RTD tea are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for D09020055 as of 2026. calcium_mg and iron_mg are set to 0.0 per HPB source (confirmed zero). GI of 47 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per 500 ml serving = (47 × 27.55) / 100 ≈ 13.0 (low-medium boundary). 'Less sugar' labelling does not exempt this product from SSB classification under Singapore HPB and WHO criteria when sugar exceeds 5 g/100 ml.
